exercises.title: Match streaming type to use case
exercises.instructions:
Match each gRPC streaming type to the correct Zerodha/Razorpay use case:
A: rpc GetQuote(QuoteRequest) returns (Quote)
B: rpc WatchPrices(WatchRequest) returns (stream Quote)
C: rpc UploadTrades(stream TradeRecord) returns (BatchSummary)
D: rpc LiveOrderBook(stream OrderRequest) returns (stream OrderUpdate)
exercises.expected_solution:
A: UNARY — GetQuote
   Single request: 'Give me current RELIANCE price'
   Single response: Quote(RELIANCE, 2800.50)
   Use: one-off lookups, simple queries
   Equivalent: REST GET /api/quotes/RELIANCE

B: SERVER STREAMING — WatchPrices
   Single request: 'Subscribe to RELIANCE live prices'
   Stream of responses: server pushes updates every 100ms
   Use: live market data feed (Zerodha Kite watchlist)
   Key: server calls onNext() repeatedly, never onCompleted() (infinite)

C: CLIENT STREAMING — UploadTrades
   Stream of requests: client sends 10,000 trade records
   Single response: BatchSummary(totalTrades=10000, totalValue=₹5Cr, errors=3)
   Use: batch trade upload, bulk payment processing (Razorpay bulk payouts)
   Key: server accumulates in onNext(), responds in client's onCompleted()

D: BIDIRECTIONAL STREAMING — LiveOrderBook
   Stream of requests: client sends new orders continuously
   Stream of responses: server sends order book updates continuously
   Use: interactive trading, live chat, collaborative editing
   Key: both sides stream independently — not request/response

test_cases.expected:
A: UNARY — GetQuote
   Single request: 'Give me current RELIANCE price'
   Single response: Quote(RELIANCE, 2800.50)
   Use: one-off lookups, simple queries
   Equivalent: REST GET /api/quotes/RELIANCE

B: SERVER STREAMING — WatchPrices
   Single request: 'Subscribe to RELIANCE live prices'
   Stream of responses: server pushes updates every 100ms
   Use: live market data feed (Zerodha Kite watchlist)
   Key: server calls onNext() repeatedly, never onCompleted() (infinite)

C: CLIENT STREAMING — UploadTrades
   Stream of requests: client sends 10,000 trade records
   Single response: BatchSummary(totalTrades=10000, totalValue=₹5Cr, errors=3)
   Use: batch trade upload, bulk payment processing (Razorpay bulk payouts)
   Key: server accumulates in onNext(), responds in client's onCompleted()

D: BIDIRECTIONAL STREAMING — LiveOrderBook
   Stream of requests: client sends new orders continuously
   Stream of responses: server sends order book updates continuously
   Use: interactive trading, live chat, collaborative editing
   Key: both sides stream independently — not request/response

hints: Server streaming: server controls when to send
Client streaming: server waits for all client messages before responding
Bidi: both sides stream independently

exercises.title: Complete the server streaming service
exercises.instructions: Fill in the server streaming implementation for Zerodha market data.

blanks.hint: generated ImplBase class matches the service name in .proto
parameter type for gRPC streaming responses
is___(): returns true when client has disconnected

hints: ServiceNameImplBase: generated base class from protoc
setOnCancelHandler: cleanup callback on disconnect
isCancelled(): essential check — prevents resource leaks

exercises.title: Server streaming with TestStreamObserver
exercises.instructions:
Write a server streaming service test:
1. QuoteStreamService: sends N quotes then completes
2. TestStreamObserver: captures all onNext values and onCompleted
3. Test: 5 quotes received, onCompleted called
4. @SpringBootTest: 2 tests pass
hints: TestStreamObserver: reuse across all streaming tests
received.size(): total messages sent by server
completed: verifies onCompleted() was called

exercises.title: Client streaming — batch trade aggregation
exercises.instructions:
Write a client streaming service:
1. Server receives stream of TradeRecord messages
2. Accumulates: totalCount, totalValuePaise, errorCount
3. In client's onCompleted: send UploadSummary, complete
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): 3 trades → correct summary, empty stream → empty summary
hints: Client streaming: server returns StreamObserver (receives client messages)
onNext() accumulates; onCompleted() sends final response
AtomicInteger/AtomicLong: thread-safe accumulation

exercises.title: Bidirectional streaming — echo service
exercises.instructions:
Write a simple bidirectional streaming service:
1. Server echoes each message back with a timestamp
2. EchoRequest(message) → EchoResponse(message, processedAt)
3. Multiple clients send simultaneously
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): echo works, multiple messages echoed
hints: Bidi: onNext() in client StreamObserver → respond via responseObserver.onNext()
Mirror completion: client's onCompleted() → server calls responseObserver.onCompleted()
Real-time: responses sent immediately without waiting for all client messages

exercises.title: Cancel handler and resource cleanup
exercises.instructions:
Write server streaming with proper cleanup:
1. MarketSubscriptionService: tracks active subscriptions
2. On stream start: add to subscription map
3. onCancelHandler: remove from map (client disconnected)
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): subscription added on start, removed on cancel
hints: ConcurrentHashMap: thread-safe subscription registry
setOnCancelHandler: key cleanup when client disconnects (prevents memory leak)
isCancelled() check in loop: stop sending to dead connections

exercises.title: Zerodha live price feed — server streaming
exercises.instructions:
Build Zerodha live NSE price feed:
1. PriceFeedService: watchPrices(symbol, interval) sends N quotes
2. Each quote: symbol + random price ±0.5% of base
3. isCancelled check in loop
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): correct count, prices near base, no errors
hints: Random walk: each price ±0.5% of last (not base) for realistic movement
isCancelled: loop condition checks before each send
BASE_PRICES default 1000.0: handles unknown symbols gracefully

exercises.title: Razorpay batch payment — client streaming
exercises.instructions:
Build Razorpay batch payment uploader:
1. Client sends stream of PaymentInstruction (merchantId, amount, upi)
2. Server validates each: rejects if amount=0 or upi blank
3. In onCompleted: send BatchResult(total, processed, failed, totalValuePaise)
4. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): 5 valid + 2 invalid → correct counts
hints: Client streaming server: accumulate in onNext(), respond in onCompleted()
AtomicInteger/AtomicLong: thread-safe accumulators (gRPC may use multiple threads)
totalValue excludes failed: only count successfully processed payments

exercises.title: Bidirectional order book streaming
exercises.instructions:
Build live order book with bidirectional streaming:
1. OrderBookService: receives BUY/SELL orders, sends order book updates
2. Each client order → update book → send new book state to client
3. OrderBook: simple bid/ask tracking
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): buy order updates book, book state returned
hints: Bidi: immediate response per incoming message (not batch)
ConcurrentSkipListMap with reverseOrder: sorted bids (highest first)
Spread: bestAsk - bestBid = market spread (Zerodha shows this on Kite)

exercises.title: Back-pressure analysis
exercises.instructions:
Analyze these server streaming scenarios and explain when onNext() might cause problems:

Scenario A: 100 clients subscribed to RELIANCE live prices. Server sends 1,000 quotes/sec per client. Client processes at 800 quotes/sec.
Scenario B: Server calls onNext() 1,000 times in a tight loop without any delay.
Scenario C: Client has unstable mobile connection (drops for 10 seconds then reconnects).
Scenario D: Server checks isReady() before each onNext().
exercises.expected_solution:
Scenario A: Client processes slower than server sends (100 backlog)
   Problem: server's gRPC output buffer fills up
   After buffer full: onNext() blocks (waiting for client to drain buffer)
   If server is single-threaded: other clients starved while waiting
   Fix:
     - Check isReady() before sending
     - setOnReadyHandler(): get callback when client buffer is drained
     - Rate limit: don't send faster than client can receive
     - Client sends flow control: 'stop sending' via cancel

Scenario B: 1,000 onNext() calls in tight loop
   Problem: entire buffer filled in milliseconds
   Memory: 1,000 messages × 100 clients = 100,000 in-memory messages
   Risk: OutOfMemoryError if buffer unbounded
   Network: burst of data may overwhelm TCP
   Fix: add isReady() check + setOnReadyHandler pause/resume
   Pattern:
     if (serverObs.isReady()) { obs.onNext(msg); }
     else { queue.add(msg); } // pause until ready

Scenario C: Mobile connection drops for 10 seconds
   gRPC detects connection drop via HTTP/2 ping frames
   After timeout: connection marked cancelled
   isCancelled() returns true on next check
   setOnCancelHandler() fires: cleanup subscription
   If no cancel check: server sends into dead connection for 10s
   Fix: isCancelled() check + setOnCancelHandler() mandatory

Scenario D: Check isReady() before each onNext()
   Correct pattern:
     if (serverObs.isReady()) {
         serverObs.onNext(quote);
     } else {
         // Queue for later, set ready handler to resume
         serverObs.setOnReadyHandler(this::flushQueue);
     }
   Prevents: buffer overflow, memory leaks, OOM
   isReady() = false: client's gRPC buffer is full (slow consumer)
   setOnReadyHandler: callback when buffer drains (client caught up)

test_cases.expected:
Scenario A: Client processes slower than server sends (100 backlog)
   Problem: server's gRPC output buffer fills up
   After buffer full: onNext() blocks (waiting for client to drain buffer)
   If server is single-threaded: other clients starved while waiting
   Fix:
     - Check isReady() before sending
     - setOnReadyHandler(): get callback when client buffer is drained
     - Rate limit: don't send faster than client can receive
     - Client sends flow control: 'stop sending' via cancel

Scenario B: 1,000 onNext() calls in tight loop
   Problem: entire buffer filled in milliseconds
   Memory: 1,000 messages × 100 clients = 100,000 in-memory messages
   Risk: OutOfMemoryError if buffer unbounded
   Network: burst of data may overwhelm TCP
   Fix: add isReady() check + setOnReadyHandler pause/resume
   Pattern:
     if (serverObs.isReady()) { obs.onNext(msg); }
     else { queue.add(msg); } // pause until ready

Scenario C: Mobile connection drops for 10 seconds
   gRPC detects connection drop via HTTP/2 ping frames
   After timeout: connection marked cancelled
   isCancelled() returns true on next check
   setOnCancelHandler() fires: cleanup subscription
   If no cancel check: server sends into dead connection for 10s
   Fix: isCancelled() check + setOnCancelHandler() mandatory

Scenario D: Check isReady() before each onNext()
   Correct pattern:
     if (serverObs.isReady()) {
         serverObs.onNext(quote);
     } else {
         // Queue for later, set ready handler to resume
         serverObs.setOnReadyHandler(this::flushQueue);
     }
   Prevents: buffer overflow, memory leaks, OOM
   isReady() = false: client's gRPC buffer is full (slow consumer)
   setOnReadyHandler: callback when buffer drains (client caught up)

hints: isReady() = false: client buffer full — pause sending
isCancelled() = true: connection dead — stop and cleanup
setOnReadyHandler: resume sending callback when buffer drains

exercises.title: Complete Zerodha market streaming service
exercises.instructions:
Build complete Zerodha market streaming:
1. SymbolRegistry: add/remove symbol subscriptions per client
2. Server streaming: watchPrices sends N quotes, isCancelled check, onCancel cleanup
3. Stats: totalMessagesStreamed, activeSubscriptions
4. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): stream count, cancel cleanup, stats, error on empty symbol
hints: registry.remove() twice: onCancelHandler + after loop (idempotent)
totalStreamed tracks all successful messages sent
Gaussian random walk: more realistic than uniform random

exercises.title: Client streaming with validation and error propagation
exercises.instructions:
Razorpay bulk payout with per-record validation:
1. Process each PaymentInstruction: validate, process, or accumulate error
2. If any instruction has fatal error (null merchantId): stop stream with onError
3. Non-fatal errors (invalid amount): accumulate in failedList
4. onCompleted: send BatchResult with success + failures
5. @SpringBootTest (3 tests): partial failures, fatal error stops stream, all-valid
hints: Fatal vs non-fatal: null merchantId = DATA_LOSS (stop stream); invalid amount = accumulate
fatalError.get() guard: stop processing after fatal (don't call responseObserver again)
Status.DATA_LOSS: signals data integrity problem (not just invalid request)

exercises.title: Complete bidirectional trade streaming
exercises.instructions:
Build complete bidi trading service:
1. LiveTradingService: receives order events (new order, cancel, modify)
2. Sends acknowledgements and book snapshots
3. OrderBook: bids + asks sorted maps
4. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): new order acked, cancel handled, book updates, empty symbol error
hints: Bidi: each onNext from client triggers immediate onNext to client
ConcurrentSkipListMap: sorted, thread-safe bid/ask book
CANCEL type: remove from activeOrders (simplified — real would need orderId lookup)

exercises.title: isReady back-pressure implementation
exercises.instructions:
Implement back-pressure with isReady():
1. BackPressureStreamService: queues messages when not ready
2. setOnReadyHandler: drains queue when client is ready
3. Counter: messagesQueued, messagesSent
4. @SpringBootTest (2 tests): messages sent via ready, queued when not ready
hints: isReady(): true = client buffer has space; false = client is slow
setOnReadyHandler: callback when client drains buffer (resume sending)
pendingQueue: buffer pending messages during backpressure

exercises.title: Complete M28 T3 gRPC Streaming synthesis
exercises.instructions:
Build complete M28 T3 synthesis:
1. Server streaming (5 quotes, isCancelled check)
2. Client streaming (accumulate, respond in onCompleted)
3. Bidi streaming (echo with timestamp)
4. Back-pressure (isReady check)
5. @SpringBootTest (4 tests): all streaming types work
6. All 4 pass
exercises.starter_code: // Complete M28 T3 gRPC Streaming synthesis
hints: 0 quotes: loop doesn't execute → onCompleted still called → valid
client total 1+2=3: seq numbers summed
bidi seq*2: simple transformation proves processing happened
